title: MAS to boost Singapore equities market with education, guardrails and a risk‑taking framework
body: MAS deputy chairman Chee Hong Tat announced increased resources for public and investor education, new guardrails and a more risk‑taking approach to revive Singapore’s equities market, while emphasizing transparency, shareholder engagement and stronger enforcement. He also responded to Workers’ Party proposals, noting that some value‑creation disclosures may become mandatory but stressing that not all measures should be prescribed.

title: SGX posts strongest half‑year results since 2000, driven by higher trading volumes and IPO activity
body: SGX reported a half‑year net profit of $342.7 million (up 0.8%) and revenue of $736.2 million (up 7.9%), with daily average securities trading value rising 20% to $1.51 billion—the highest in five years, and an interim dividend of 21.75 cents per share, up 20.8% year‑on‑year. The exchange attributed the performance to stronger multi‑asset trading, a robust IPO pipeline of more than 30 companies and the Equity Market Development Programme supported by the Monetary Authority of Singapore.

title: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding fell about 5.6% after a drop in Maersk earnings and job‑cut plans, pulling Singapore's Straits Times Index down 0.7%
body: Yangzijiang Shipbuilding’s shares slid 5.3% to 5.6% in early trading, erasing over S$700 million in market value, after Maersk reported lower earnings and announced plans to cut 1,000 jobs, with Maersk shares down 3.7% in Copenhagen. The broader market saw the STI down 0.7% and declines in DBS (‑0.7%), OCBC (‑0.9%) and Singtel (‑0.8%), while regional indices and US tech stocks also fell.

title: SGX posts modest profit rise, raises interim dividend and highlights strong IPO pipeline
body: SGX reported H1 FY2026 adjusted net profit of S$357.1 million (up 11.6%) with revenue of S$736.2 million and declared an interim dividend of S$0.11 per share payable on Feb 24. CEO Loh Boon Chye said trading broadened across sectors, institutional investors bought S$415 million of small‑ and mid‑cap stocks, the exchange raised nearly S$3 billion from IPOs and is preparing a Global Listing Board to attract more high‑growth companies.

title: Keppel shares jump as it proposes 13‑cent dividend while M1 stake sale to Simba Telecom faces regulatory delay
body: Keppel's shares rose over 6% after announcing a 13‑cent special dividend, but the expected $1.43 billion sale of its 83.9% stake in M1 to Simba Telecom is delayed pending regulatory approval, with cash proceeds of about $1 billion slated for recognition in the 2026 monetisation value. The company also highlighted the need to boost occupancy at its Keppel South Central office tower before any further divestments and outlined its early‑stage AI initiatives and competitive positioning against firms such as BlackRock and Global Infrastructure Partners.

title: Asian markets slide as AI spending worries hit Singapore’s STI and crypto prices tumble
body: The Straits Times Index fell 0.8% to 4,934.41 as investors reacted to Amazon’s $200 billion AI‑related spending plan and broader tech sell‑off, dragging down blue‑chip stocks such as Yangzijiang Shipbuilding (‑6.2%), DBS (‑0.6%), OCBC (‑1%) and Singtel (‑1.3%). Bitcoin slipped to around US$63,000, a 50% drop over three months, with Maybank analysts linking the crypto decline to rising AI costs and the nomination of hawkish Fed chair candidate Kevin Warsh, while regional indices showed mixed moves.

title: Singapore Exchange posts slight profit increase amid earnings‑season rally while Singtel, Keppel and Hongkong Land announce major deals and dividend upgrades
body: Singapore Exchange SGX reported a 0.8% rise in first‑half net profit to $342.7 million and a 7.9% revenue increase, declared a 21.75 cent interim dividend and reiterated 6‑8% medium‑term revenue growth despite a modest share dip. During the same week, Singtel teamed with KKR in a $6.6 billion acquisition of ST Telemedia Global Data Centres, Keppel posted a 39% jump in FY2025 profit to $1.1 billion and proposed a 47 cent total dividend, Hongkong Land launched an $8.2 billion Singapore Central Private Real Estate Fund, and parliament debated the effectiveness of the Equity Market Development Programme.

title: Boustead Singapore moves ahead with UI Boustead REIT listing and divests industrial assets
body: Boustead Singapore received an eligibility-to-list letter from the Singapore Exchange for UI Boustead REIT and entered definitive agreements to sell its interests in 15 industrial properties, generating about $258.7 million in proceeds, of which roughly $202.8 million will be used to subscribe for a 16.9 % stake in the REIT. Metro Holdings, through its subsidiary Metrobilt Construction, will sell its 26 % stake in Boustead Industrial Fund to the REIT for an estimated $116 million, while Boustead shares traded at $2.15, down 1.4 % on Feb 10.

title: SGX reports 66% YoY turnover rise in January as STI gains 5.6% and new listings boost activity
body: The Singapore Exchange posted a S$34.6 billion turnover in January, up 66% year‑on‑year, with securities daily average value rising 58% to S$1.6 billion and ETF assets reaching a record S$19.1 billion. The STI climbed 5.6% month‑on‑month, while new listings such as Concord New Energy Group, The Assembly Place and Toku, and fresh ETFs including UOBAM Ping An FTSE Asean Dividend Index ETF and CSOP CSAM CSI A500 Index ETF, contributed to heightened trading across equities, derivatives and commodities.

title: Mindarie Investment's cash offer for Alpha Integrated Real Estate Investment Trust AI-Reit becomes unconditional, closing date extended to Feb 24
body: Mindarie Investment's mandatory conditional cash offer for Alpha Integrated Real Estate Investment Trust AI-Reit was declared unconditional on Feb 9 after acceptances covering about 10% of units, raising Volare and its concert parties' holdings to roughly 51.2% of the REIT’s issued units. The closing date was extended to 5.30 pm on Feb 24, and UOB, the offeror’s financial adviser, said the REIT will stay listed with no material changes planned.

title: Singapore's STI breaks 5,000 points as banks rally and market reforms boost confidence
body: The Straits Times Index climbed above 5,000 on Feb 12, driven by gains in its three major banks—DBS up 0.5%, OCBC up 1.3% and UOB up 0.9%—and supported by MAS and SGX initiatives to enhance liquidity and attract listings. The rise follows a $5 billion equity market development programme, a new iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index, a dual‑listing highway with Nasdaq, and a reduced board‑lot size aimed at revitalising Singapore’s equities market.

title: OCBC downgrades Sheng Siong to hold, shares fall 4.2%
body: Sheng Siong’s shares dropped 4.2% to $2.73 after OCBC downgraded the stock to “hold,” citing a forward 12‑month P/E of 24.8 versus its 19.6 historical average. The analyst kept a defensive outlook, noted strong earnings visibility, and raised the fair‑value target to $2.89.

title: Singapore’s STI falls below 5,000 after Budget 2026 rollout as new equity‑market support measures are announced
body: The Straits Times Index slipped 1.3% to 4,949.93 points on Friday following the Budget 2026 announcements, which introduced expanded EQDP funding, a S$1.5 bn Anchor Fund tranche, a S$1 bn Startup SG Equity scheme and household cash vouchers. Analysts such as JPMorgan and Maybank remain positive, citing the fiscal surplus boost and the support for sectors including DBS, OCBC, UOB, Singtel, ST Engineering, Keppel DC Reit and other tech and consumer firms.

title: iFast reports 45.7% revenue growth and record AUA in Q4 2025, proposes higher dividend and announces stake acquisition
body: iFast posted Q4 2025 revenue of S$151.7 million, up 45.7% year‑on‑year, net profit of S$32.9 million and a final dividend of S$0,025 per share, while its banking arm iFast Global Bank achieved its first full‑year profit; the group’s assets under administration reached a record S$31.98 billion and it conditionally agreed to acquire a 30% stake in Financial Alliance Corporation to link its wealth platform with advisory services.

title: Lum Chang Creations receives SGX approval to transfer from Catalist to the mainboard after reporting a 104% profit increase
body: Lum Chang Creations was granted in‑principal approval by the Singapore Exchange to move to the mainboard, subject to shareholder approval, and reported first‑half net profit of S$11 million, up 104% year‑on‑year. Its parent, Lum Chang Holdings, holds a 71.1% stake and the company was added to the MSCI Global Micro Cap Indexes – Singapore Index effective Feb 27.

title: Singapore's STI retreats after Budget 2026 despite new equity and startup support measures
body: Following the Budget 2026 announcement, the Straits Times Index slipped 1.6% to 4,936.51 points before closing the day at 4,937.78, with bank stocks DBS, OCBC and UOB falling 1.2%‑3.1% and Singtel down 1.8%, while the government pledged an extra S$1.5 billion for the Anchor Fund, S$1 billion for the Startup SG Equity Scheme and a revised surplus of S$15.1 billion; analysts such as JPMorgan and Maybank remain bullish, citing a sustained AI boom, a fiscal buffer and sectoral benefits for firms including DBS, UOB, SGX, UOL, Keppel DC Reit, ST Engineering, Addvalue Tech, AEM, Frencken, UMS and CapitaLand Integrated Commercial Trust.

title: Singapore adds $1.5 bn to the Financial Sector Development Fund to expand the Equity Market Development Programme
body: The 2026 Budget announced a further S$1.5 bn top‑up to the Financial Sector Development Fund, boosting the $5 bn Equity Market Development Programme (EQDP) that has already attracted about $13 bn of market funding and lifted SGX main‑board market capitalisation by S$173 bn since February 2025. Industry participants including UBS, Deloitte, DBS, Maybank Securities, CMC Invest, EY and Bank of Singapore said the injection reinforces market confidence, supports more high‑quality fund managers and is part of a broader ecosystem push involving new MAS‑MTI workgroups and initiatives such as the Value Unlock programme.

title: STI climbs to 5,000 points as budget boosts equity reforms and sparks calls for unit‑share and fractional trading
body: The Straits Times Index crossed the 5,000‑point mark on 12 February alongside Finance Minister Lawrence Wong’s Budget 2026, validating JPMorgan’s earlier bull target and prompting a S$1.5 billion top‑up to the Financial Sector Development Fund for the Equity Market Development Programme that involves JPMorgan, Avanda and Fullerton; the article also urges the market to abandon odd‑lot trading in favour of unit shares and fractional investing, citing DBS and Malaysia’s recent fractional‑share launch as examples.

title: SGX faces criticism over removal of watch list and handling of zombie firms amid regional tightening
body: Singapore Exchange’s decision to drop its financial watch list has drawn criticism from market observers who warn that the lack of a mechanism to cull loss‑making “zombie” firms could harm market quality, especially on the Catalist board where 86% of listings trade below IPO price. By contrast, Korea and Japan are tightening delisting rules, and experts such as James Leong of Grasshopper Asia, Lee Ooi Keong of Clover Point Consultants and Jimmy Seet of PwC Singapore argue that stronger disclosure and governance standards are needed to balance competitiveness with investor protection.

title: SGX RegCo urges forward‑looking disclosures while outlining legal risks and governance safeguards for listed companies
body: The Singapore Exchange’s regulator released guidance encouraging forward‑guidance disclosures, stressing that statements must be based on reasonable assumptions, qualified, and overseen by the board to avoid liability under the Securities and Futures Act and Companies Act. It also noted potential adoption of U.S. safe‑harbour provisions for forward‑looking statements on the upcoming SGX‑Nasdaq Global Listing Board.

title: Singapore data‑centre REITs post strong 2025 revenue growth but share prices remain flat amid AI‑driven demand and interest‑rate concerns
body: Keppel DC REIT, Digital Core REIT and NTT DC REIT all reported double‑digit revenue gains in 2025 on AI‑related demand—Keppel DC REIT’s revenue rose 42.2% to $441.4 million with DPU up 9.8% to 10.4 cents, Digital Core REIT’s revenue jumped 72.2% to $176.2 million while its DPU held at 3.6 US cents, and NTT DC REIT saw a modest 1.7% revenue increase—but the three pure‑play REITs traded largely flat, with performance tied to future DPU growth and potential interest‑rate moves.

title: Singapore market rallies post‑Chinese New Year as DBS, OCBC and other stocks lead gains
body: The Straits Times Index rose from about 4,938 before the holiday to open above 4,986 and close over 5,017 on Feb 20, driven by gains in DBS Bank (near $58), OCBC Bank (up to $21.72), Singtel, Yangzijiang Shipbuilding, Genting Singapore and mid‑cap names such as Food Empire and Civmec. At the same time Vicom posted a 77.6% profit jump and SIA Engineering reported a 9.7% profit rise ahead of earnings season, while gold surged above $5,100 per ounce after President Trump’s Iran warning and US Q4 GDP growth missed expectations, with several Singapore firms slated to report later in the week.

title: Singapore bourse sees STI dip as UOB profit falls, while iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index climbs
body: The Straits Times Index fell 0.4% to 5,020.79 as all three local banks—UOB, DBS and OCBC—traded lower, with UOB dropping 4.1% after reporting a 7% decline in Q4 profit to S$1.41 billion and cutting its 2026 fee‑income growth outlook. Meanwhile, the iEdge Singapore Next 50 Index rose 0.4% to 1,526.98 and Yangzijiang Shipbuilding led gains on the blue‑chip index, up 2.6%.

title: UI Boustead REIT targets up to $1.02 billion in Singapore IPO, the largest since Netlink NBN Trust in 2017
body: UI Boustead REIT is offering 1.1 billion units at 88 cents each, aiming to raise up to $1.02 billion (including a $48.1 million overallotment) and would be valued at about $1.2 billion, with cornerstone investors such as Amova Asset Management Asia, JPMorgan Asset Management Singapore, Amundi’s Singapore and Malaysia entities and Jumbo Group of Restaurants committing $377.7 million. The REIT, which holds 23 logistics and industrial assets in Singapore and Japan, projects a 7.8% distribution yield for the year ending March 31 2027 and is set to list on the Singapore Exchange on March 12.

title: Singapore's STI drops 0.9% as banks slide and Seatrium posts profit surge
body: The Straits Times Index fell 0.9% to 4,964.38, with all three local banks—DBS, OCBC and UOB—closing in the red, while Seatrium rose 3.2% after reporting a 48.3% jump in H2 net profit to $179.3 million and a full‑year profit of $323.6 million. Singapore’s factory output surged 16.6% YoY in January, but regional markets were mixed, with Hong Kong down 1.4% and Japan up 0.3%.
